title: quercus glandulifera var. donarium
intro: Quercus glandulifera var. donarium, also known as Korean oak or Japanese white oak, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus glandulifera var. donarium include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or taken in capsule form to harness its medicinal properties.
